what_it_does: Builds and queries a mechanical provenance graph (crossings) over a SQLite conversation store: it links files to exchanges via produced_by/references edges, links adjacent exchanges in a session via precedes, and links project>session>exchange via contains, using chunked keyset pagination and idempotent writes. It also exposes read traversals for what touched a file, what a session touched, and what artefacts an exchange produced or referenced.
observations: Uses keyset pagination and one transaction per chunk to avoid materializing millions of rows and to avoid per-row autocommit overhead.
Canonicalizes artefact paths before creating file nodes, expanding ~ and resolving relative paths against the exchange's cwd, and flags paths that cannot be canonicalized.
Uses a COALESCE of file_path and notebook_path so NotebookEdit artefact addresses are included.
Distinguishes producing tools (Write/Edit/MultiEdit/NotebookEdit) from referencing tools (Read) for artefact edges.
Counts and asserts a closed sum: every tool_call in scope is either crossed or recorded as a gap, throwing if the counts mismatch.
Carries the previous exchange across temporal page boundaries so adjacency edges straddling pages are not lost.
Includes an honest note that the json_extract expression index is forward-looking and does not accelerate current queries.
Leaves a clearly marked seam for future semantic, model-judged cross-project concept links.
standouts: The executable closed-sum assertion that fails loud if any tool_call is unaccounted.
The mechanical-only scope with explicit reservation of grade/confidence columns for a later semantic lane.
The cross-page prev carry in the temporal phase to preserve adjacency across chunk boundaries.
conventions: Heavy JSDoc with architectural rationale and scope caveats.
Constants for tool lists and chunk size; type interfaces for all result shapes.
Uses imported deterministic node-id helpers (fileNodeId, sessionNodeId, projectNodeId) from db.ts.
One db.transaction per page of rows, read-then-write pattern.
Alias export for artefactsProducedBy pointing to artefactsOfExchange.
concerns: The whatTouchedFile lookup canonicalizes with a null exchange cwd, so relative query paths may not match build-time canonicalization that used the exchange cwd.
artefactsOfExchange returns the produced edge type as the literal string 'produces' even though the stored edge_type is 'produced_by'; this relies on the registry inverse convention being stable.
The temporal ordering key (session_id, timestamp, line_start) may not be unique if timestamps collide and line_start duplicates within a session, which could drop or mis-order adjacency edges.
The containment phase writes a project->session edge once per exchange, relying on idempotent insertLink to avoid duplicate counting; this is correct but generates repeated attempts.
notes: A scale-aware, mechanical provenance graph builder that insists on accounting for every tool_call and leaves semantic linking as a future add-on.
questions: What is the exact schema of the relation_type registry and how does insertLink enforce unknown kinds?
How are fileNodeId, sessionNodeId, and projectNodeId formatted, and do they match the exchange node grammar used here?
What proportion of tool_calls have a null or relative cwd, and how are those uncanonicalized paths surfaced to operators?
How will the planned semantic lane generate candidate near-pairs and what judge will ratify confidence scores?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it builds a mechanical provenance/crossings graph and read traversals over the existing store, with semantic linking explicitly deferred.
apparent_themes: No silent failures and explicit gap accounting.
Registry-driven relation types instead of code-side enums.
Clear separation between mechanical and semantic edge lanes.
intention_signals: Derive a deterministic, idempotent, and auditable graph of who touched what, when, and in which project/session context, while keeping judgement-based semantic links out of this lane.
novelty: The closed-sum gap accounting with an executable assertion, the honest disclaimer about an unused expression index, and the cross-page prev carry for temporal adjacency.
contribution: Provides a chunked, idempotent builder for mechanical provenance edges and three read traversals over them, plus path canonicalization and explicit gap accounting, with a reserved seam for later semantic links.
summary_for_embedding: A TypeScript module that builds and queries a mechanical provenance graph linking files, exchanges, sessions, and projects in a SQLite store via chunked, idempotent edge insertion.
intention_for_embedding: To derive a deterministic, scale-safe graph of artefact touches and temporal/containment relationships from existing conversation store records without model judgement.

symbols.does: Normalizes artefact paths and resolves relative paths against an exchange cwd, flagging paths that cannot be canonicalized.
symbols.description: Expands leading ~ to the home directory, normalizes absolute paths, and resolves relative paths against the provided exchange cwd. If a relative path has no usable base, it returns the verbatim path with canonical=false so the caller can flag it rather than mis-resolve it.

symbols.does: Aggregates counts and notes returned by the graph build pass.
symbols.description: A type/interface that records how many produced, referenced, temporal, and containment edges were inserted, the total new links, the gap count and note, the tool_calls in scope, and any uncanonicalized paths.

symbols.does: Creates an expression index on the extracted artefact path in tool_calls if it does not exist.
symbols.description: Idempotently runs CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS over a COALESCE json_extract expression for file_path/notebook_path. The file notes this index is forward-looking and does not currently accelerate the build or traversal queries.

symbols.does: Builds the canonical exchange node address string from session/id and line_start.
symbols.description: Returns exchange://<session_id_or_exchange_id>/<line_start>, matching the provenance address grammar so the graph can refer to exchanges without materializing full exchange objects.

symbols.does: Main batch builder that inserts all mechanical edges for a project, session, or whole store.
symbols.description: Keyset-paginates tool_calls and exchanges in chunks, writes each page in a transaction, asserts artefact, temporal, and containment edges via insertLink, and enforces a closed-sum identity that every tool_call is either crossed or counted as a gap.

symbols.does: Describes one exchange that produced or referenced a file.
symbols.description: A type/interface carrying relation direction, the exchange node address, session, project, timestamp, and edge type for results from the file traversal.

symbols.does: Returns every exchange that produced or referenced a given file.
symbols.description: Canonicalizes the query path, looks up produced_by and references edges touching the file node, joins back to the exchanges table to recover session/project/timestamp, and returns ordered FileTouch records.

symbols.does: Describes one file produced or referenced by an exchange within a session.
symbols.description: A type/interface carrying relation direction, the decoded file path, file node id, exchange node address, edge type, and timestamp for results from the session traversal.

symbols.does: Returns every file produced or referenced by any exchange in a session.
symbols.description: Walks from the session node down through contains edges to its exchanges, then out through produced_by and references edges to file nodes, decoding file paths and returning ordered SessionTouch records.

symbols.does: Describes one artefact produced or referenced by a single exchange.
symbols.description: A type/interface carrying relation direction, decoded file path, file node id, edge type, and timestamp for results from the exchange traversal.

symbols.does: Returns all artefacts produced or referenced by a given exchange node.
symbols.description: Looks up produced_by edges where the exchange is the target and references edges where it is the source, decodes file node ids to absolute paths, and returns ExchangeArtefact records.
